assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and can run curl.
No Mux organization, environment access token, or asset exist yet.
A publicly reachable sample video URL may be used; Mux documents demo URLs.

developer_goal: Create Mux access, ingest a sample video, wait until it is ready, and watch the streamed result.

first_success.outcome: A publicly playable Mux asset reaches ready state and can be watched through its playback ID.
first_success.observable_signal: Opening https://stream.new/v/{PLAYBACK_ID} (or an equivalent documented player URL) streams the uploaded video.

route_selection.surface: Mux docs Stream videos in five minutes
route_selection.rule: Prefer the core get-started on-demand guide; within it prefer the complete curl request path over language SDK tabs.
route_selection.selected: Stream videos in five minutes using dashboard access token and curl

candidates.name: Stream videos in five minutes (curl / HTTP)
candidates.reason: Docs present this as the core upload-and-playback get-started route with an explicit five-minute claim and stream.new preview.

candidates.name: Same guide via official language SDKs
candidates.reason: SDK tabs add package installation for the same asset-create outcome; curl is a complete request without language package setup.

candidates.name: Mux live streaming starter
candidates.reason: Separate live-broadcast outcome; on-demand Stream videos in five minutes is the core get-started path.

required_path.action: Create a Mux account and open the dashboard.

required_fields.label: Mux account registration fields
required_fields.notes: The five-minute guide begins at Access Token settings inside an existing dashboard; public signup HTML did not expose the registration field set.

required_path.observable_result: The developer can open Access Token settings for an environment.

required_path.action: Generate an Access Token with Mux Video Read and Write permissions and save the Token ID and Secret.

required_fields.label: Mux Video Read and Write permissions
Access Token name
required_fields.notes: Guide treats the internal name as optional guidance.

required_path.observable_result: Token ID and Secret Key are displayed for use as Basic auth.

required_path.action: POST a public video URL to the Mux assets endpoint using Basic authentication with the token pair.
required_path.observable_result: Mux returns an asset including an Asset ID and Playback ID with status preparing.

required_path.action: After the asset status becomes ready, open stream.new/v/{PLAYBACK_ID} to watch the video.
required_path.observable_result: The browser streams the ingested video.

prerequisites.description: A publicly accessible input video URL, or one of the demo URLs Mux documents.
curl or another HTTP client capable of authenticated POST requests.

unavoidable_waits.description: Mux must download and process the asset until status becomes ready before playback should be attempted.

platform_outcomes.description: Mux creates an asset in preparing state and issues playback IDs.
When processing finishes, asset status becomes ready for stream.mux.com / stream.new playback.

excluded.reason: Optional tabs for the same asset-create step; curl completes the request without package install.
Documented as the best notification method, while manual polling is allowed at low volume.
Presented as a next step after playback works.

excluded.item: Reading documentation as a counted action
excluded.reason: Documentation is evidence, not a product interaction.

sources.title: Stream videos in five minutes
evidence_supported: Access token generation fields and permissions
Playback ID and stream.new preview terminal
SDK tabs as alternatives to direct HTTP

uncertainties.question: What exact Mux account registration and organization-creation fields precede Access Token settings for a brand-new user?
uncertainties.impact: The guide's five-minute path starts after dashboard access, so account-boundary field counts remain unverified and the audit cannot be marked verified.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated walkthrough from Mux signup through first Access Token generation.

uncertainties.question: Does the five-minute claim include account creation?
uncertainties.impact: Vendor time claims cannot be interpreted for cold-start developers until account steps are inventoried.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Clarify whether marketing timing starts at token creation or at account registration.
